channel.title: Elliott Bay Book Company Events
channel.description: Upcoming events for Elliott Bay Book Company Events

item.title: Ryan Cahill w/ Terry Brooks & BookBorn, OF BLOOD AND FIRE
item.description:
Sunday, March 8, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

An epic adventure with dragons, elves, dwarves, gods, and magic, set in a world where good people struggle to protect what they love

Event details

item.title: Richard Hell, GODLIKE
item.description:
Monday, March 9, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

A tribute to poetry and the beauty and mess of art, desire, and New York City.

Event details

item.title: Rachael DeVaux, THE HIGH-PROTEIN PLATE
item.description:
Tuesday, March 10, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Rachael DeVaux, author of Rachael’s Good Eats, shows how anyone at any stage of life can easily incorporate protein into their diet

Event details

item.title: Tom Crestodina, WORKING BOATS: SAFETY, SALVAGE, AND RESCUE & SALMON TROLLER
item.description:
Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Tom visits for his new boat books!

Event details

item.title: Eirinie Carson, BLOODFIRE, BABY
item.description:
Thursday, March 12, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

A gothic tale following one woman’s fight to keep her baby safe when ancestral shadow figures emerge with a cryptic message.

Event details

item.title: Scott Broker w/ Mattilda Bernstein Sycamore, THE DISAPPOINTMENT
item.description:
Friday, March 13, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

'The Disappointment' explores the mutual exhilaration and terror of being placed center stage in one’s own life.

Event details

item.title: Peregrine Press presents Felwine Sarr & Malick Ndiaye
item.description:
Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Discussing two major works of African literature in English translation, 'Dahij' by Sarr and 'Letters to My President' by Ndiaye

Event details

item.title: Janée J. Baugher, THE ANDREW WYETH CHRONICLES
item.description:
Sunday, March 15, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

An imaginative narrative of the painter’s creative life, rife with both losses and pleasures

Event details

item.title: Dr. Terence Keel, THE CORONER'S SILENCE
item.description:
Monday, March 16, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

A landmark investigation into forensic medicine exposing systematic concealment of state-sanctioned violence through death investigations

Event details

item.title: Wendy Martin w/ Miranda Mundt, CASTLE SWIMMER: VOL 3
item.description:
Tuesday, March 17, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Kappa and Siren’s adventurous journey into the vast ocean begins!

Event details

item.title: T Kira Madden w/ Danya Kukafka, WHIDBEY
item.description:
Wednesday, March 18, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

A portrait of three women connected through one man in the aftermath of his murder

Event details

item.title: Lauren Westerfield w/ Erin Langner & Katie Lee Ellison, WOMAN HOUSE
item.description:
Thursday, March 19, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Discussing Westerfield's new book 'Woman House: Essays and Assemblages'

Event details

item.title: Tayari Jones w/ Ijeoma Oluo, KIN
item.description:
Monday, March 23, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Emotionally rich, unforgettable work from one of the brightest and most irresistible voices in contemporary fiction

Event details

item.title: Jane Hirshfield, THE ASKING
item.description:
Tuesday, March 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Drawing from new work, nine previous books, and five decades of writing

Event details

item.title: Hannah Lillith Assadi, PARADISO 17
item.description:
Wednesday, March 25, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Inspired by the life of her late Palestinian father, which follows one man’s restless search for home the world over

Event details

item.title: Rachel Griffin, THE SUN AND THE STARMAKER
item.description:
Thursday, March 26, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

A meet and greet with Rachel Griffin!

Event details

item.title: Virtual: Louise Erdrich w/ Ann Patchett, PYTHON'S KISS
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: 1521 10th Ave, Seattle, WA 98122

From Pulitzer Prize and National Book Award-winning author Louise Erdrich, a captivating collection of short stories

Event details

item.title: From the Natural World w/ Nadine Maestas, M. Elliott, & Vitoria Ramos
item.description:
Friday, March 27, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Elliott Bay Book Co. and Gamut present OFFICE HOURS: A Reading Series Readings from the Natural World

Event details

item.title: Rebecca Brown w/ Christopher Frizzelle, MY ANIMAL KINGDOM
item.description:
Saturday, March 28, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Rebecca Brown adopted a baby squirrel, and it changed her life.

Event details

item.title: Bootsy Holler w/ Troy Nelson, MAKING IT
item.description:
Monday, March 30, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

'Making It: An Intimate Documentary of the Seattle Indie, Rock & Punk Scene, 1992-2008'

Event details

item.title: Kim Samek w/ Josh Riedel, I AM THE GHOST HERE: STORIES
item.description:
Tuesday, March 31, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

In which twelve women confront the mounting existential terrors of modern life

Event details

item.title: Ashley McGirt w/ Marcus Harrison Green, THE COST OF HEALING IN SILENCE
item.description:
Wednesday, April 1, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

An up-to-date and expert discussion of how to create a more culturally responsive mental health care system

Event details

item.title: J.M. Sidorova w/ Rashida J. Smith, THE WITCH OF PRAGUE
item.description:
Thursday, April 2, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

A work of magical realism set during the 1968 Prague Spring about bodily autonomy and the fight against authoritarian regimes everywhere.

Event details

item.title: Tracy Deonn, LEGENDBORN (Collector's Edition) & OATHBOUND (paperback)
item.description:
Friday, April 3, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

Bestselling YA author Tracy Deonn visits the store for the release of 'Legendborn' Collector's Edition and the paperback of 'Oathbound'

Event details

item.title: Lily Brooks-Dalton, RUINS
item.description:
Monday, April 6, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

“Lily Brooks-Dalton's novels are rich literary feasts." —Geraldine Brooks, bestselling author of 'Horse'

Event details

item.title: Marcus Kliewer w/ Sadie Hartmann, THE CARETAKER
item.description:
Friday, April 24, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

What starts as a peculiar side gig soon becomes a waking nightmare...

Event details

item.title: David Szalay, FLESH
item.description:
Monday, April 27, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: The Elliott Bay Book Company, 1521 10th Avenue, Seattle, WA, 98122

The winner of the Booker Prize 2025 visits the store!

Event details
